nodes.label: P4.5 — continual (A6) across difficulty
nodes.rationale: THE win, decisively difficulty-robust: OURS-sleep BWT stays -0.02 to -0.18 at every difficulty and on digits (reproducing P3.3 exactly), while online-BP catastrophically forgets (BWT -0.83 to -0.99) and a no-sleep control rots — proving sleep, not SCFF alone, carries the recovery.

nodes.label: A6 — THE continual win, robust across difficulty (the architecture's reason for being)
nodes.rationale: OURS with contrast+coordination+sleep forgets almost nothing (BWT -0.02 to -0.18) at every synthetic difficulty and on the digits home anchor (reproducing P3.3 exactly, BWT -0.017), while online-BP catastrophically forgets everywhere (BWT -0.83 to -0.99) — the largest, cleanest margin in Phase 4, later promoted as the P5.7 continual-safety gate.

nodes.label: Sleep re-fit, not SCFF alone, carries the continual recovery
nodes.rationale: SCFF's unsupervised per-sample contrast adds discriminative structure rather than overwriting old structure (no batch statistics), but the readout still drifts task-to-task; periodic sleep re-fits the readout over replayed prototypes, recovering any drift — a no-sleep control rots identically to online-BP (BWT -0.84 to -0.95), proving sleep is the decisive mechanism, not SCFF alone.
